essence: Monitors and analyses air quality to protect public health, the environment, and regulatory compliance.

identity.title: Air Quality Technician
identity.summary: Collects air‑quality data, operates monitoring equipment, analyses pollution levels, and supports environmental protection and regulatory compliance.

salary.notes: Technicians working in industrial, scientific, or government monitoring networks can exceed £55k-£65k+.

qualifications: Training in environmental science or air‑quality monitoring
Knowledge of pollution sources and atmospheric processes
Ability to operate and maintain monitoring equipment
Understanding of environmental legislation and reporting

lifestyle.description: Air quality technicians work for councils, environmental agencies, scientific labs, and industrial sites. They collect samples, maintain monitoring stations, analyse pollution data, and support environmental protection. The role suits individuals who enjoy fieldwork, data analysis, and contributing to public‑health outcomes.

habits: Works with scientists, regulators, and local authorities
